acceptance_criteria: schedule_repair.xlsx successfully parses in verifier environment
Production note includes exact sheet/cell references for all core formulas and mappings
Option-effect mapping implemented as explicit cells referenced by decision table
Verification log artifact demonstrates parser success and lists the key formula cells and named ranges
schedule_repair.xlsx successfully parses in verifier environment
Production note includes exact sheet/cell references for all core formulas and mappings
Option-effect mapping implemented as explicit cells referenced by decision table
Verification log artifact demonstrates parser success and lists the key formula cells and named ranges

test_instructions: Before finalizing the revised attempt, run the provided parse-and-verify script in the workspace to produce a verification log. Attach the log as attempts/revised/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]

Verifiers should confirm the verification_log.json shows that openpyxl successfully read: named ranges, observed_risk_score formula cell, option-effect cells, and that the decision table cells point to those computed values.

planned_changes: Open schedule_repair.xlsx in a local environment using the same parser expected by the verifier (openpyxl) to confirm parseability; fix any file format issues if needed and save a parser-compatible .xlsx.
Add a 'data_mapping' sheet that defines named ranges for each packet value (e.g., R1_proc_h, R1_due_h, ... R4_net_value) and documents the exact mapping to input/task.json rows.

Implement explicit spreadsheet formulas for normalization and observed_risk_score on a calculation sheet; reference those formula cells from the final decision table via cell links rather than duplicating the formula text in the note.

Create cells that encode per-option margin improvement points (O1, O2, O3) and reference those cells in the post-repair margin calculations. Do not rely solely on narrative in the production_control_note.
Annotate key cells (via cell comments or a 'verification' column) with the input JSON citation used (record id and field) so automated graders can trace numbers to the packet.

Run an automated parse-and-verify script in the attempt workspace that (a) imports the workbook with openpyxl, (b) reads the named ranges and key formula cells, and (c) prints a short JSON verification log; include that log as an artifact in the revised attempt.

Update production_control_note.md to include sheet names and exact cell addresses for: normalization constants, observed_risk_score formula cell, threshold cell, classification band cells, option-effect cells, and the final decision table cells.

Add a 1-2 paragraph reconciliation in production_control_note.md describing why the initial grader awarded semantic credit (if applicable) and confirming that workbook inspection now supports (or corrects) previously stated semantic claims.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Make the workbook verifiable and remove ambiguities that prevented semantic verification; ensure grader/verifier evidence alignment.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Make the workbook verifiable and remove ambiguities that prevented semantic verification; ensure grader/verifier evidence alignment.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The submission includes all required artifacts and documents clear assumptions, decision rules, sensitivity checks, and a budget-valid recommendation. However, the verifier was unable to parse the schedule_repair.xlsx workbook in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), which means key semantic claims about workbook formulas, cell-level mappings, and row-level citations are unverified. The model grader awarded full semantic credit despite this parse limitation, producing an internal inconsistency with the deterministic precheck metadata that explicitly notes semantic correctness was not evaluated. Action required: make the workbook verifiable and eliminate ambiguity around how option effects map to margin improvements; provide explicit, cell-level formula references and named ranges so verifiers can confirm implementation and re-award semantic credit.
